checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?

findings.detail: The plan still leaves critical pinned-group lifecycle behavior as open assumptions, especially manual deletion or manual movement of member clips, but the current renderer and final-video switcher both require every stored `clipId` to remain present and co-located; in `TimelineCanvas` missing or out-of-order actions cause the group overlay to disappear entirely, so this is not just a product question but an implementation requirement.

The plan reframes the menu as generic "Switch to Video/Images", but the current UI only exposes switching when `menu.hasFinalVideo` is true and the caller in `TimelineEditor` looks up `finalVideoMap.get(group.shotId)` before invoking the switch. If that availability guard is not preserved, the new menu would advertise a switch path for shots that have no final video asset.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?

findings.detail: The proposed suppression key of `${shotId}:${trackId}` is too coarse for this repository. `useShotGroups()` can legitimately emit multiple separate groups for the same shot on the same row when there is a large gap or ordering break and then a later valid run starts again, so pinning one cluster would incorrectly hide unrelated suggested clusters for that same shot/track.

The metadata-only pin/unpin path is not implementable through the current `applyEdit({ type: 'config' })` contract as described. That branch only accepts a `resolvedConfig`, and `useTimelineCommit` immediately runs it through `serializeForDisk()`, which currently serializes only `output`, `tracks`, `clips`, and optional `customEffects`; there is no place for `pinnedShotGroups` to survive unless the mutation contract or resolved-config types are expanded first.

The grouped-drag step is correct in spirit but unsafe in the literal form written. `useClipDrag` computes `draggedClipIds` on pointer-down and later falls back to normal click-selection behavior on pointer-up when the drag threshold is not crossed, so auto-expanding a pinned group at pointer-down would change click semantics unless the expansion is deferred until a real drag starts.

On the AI-agent side, the code that manipulates timeline configs in `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/timeline.ts` uses `structuredClone(config)` and does not enumerate top-level keys, so extra config keys themselves are unlikely to break that tool surface once the shared type and validation layers allow them.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?

findings.detail: Phase 6 scopes shot drops mostly as a `useExternalDrop` change, but the broader repository surface does not currently have a shot drag payload at all. Shared DnD utilities only recognize `generation`, `generation-multi`, and `file`, and there are no matching shot drag sources in the code search, so this feature spans source components plus shared DnD plumbing, not just the timeline drop target.

The persistence work is broader than the plan's Step 4 wording suggests because the repo rebuilds timeline configs in several places with fresh object literals. Besides serialization and `rowsToConfig`, `migrateToFlatTracks()` and `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry()` are also part of the normal edit/load path and must explicitly preserve the new field.

Adding an optional `pinnedShotGroups` parameter to `useShotGroups()` itself is otherwise localized. The hook only has one production caller in `TimelineEditor`, and the existing unit tests call it directly without that extra argument, so defaulting the new parameter is mechanically safe for current callers.

checks.question: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?

findings.detail: `migrateToFlatTracks()` definitely strips unknown top-level config keys today. It rebuilds `TimelineConfig` with only `output`, `tracks`, `clips`, and `customEffects`, so the plan should name this file as a required edit rather than saying to merely verify whether migration helpers strip unknown keys.

If `rowsToConfig()` gains a new `pinnedShotGroups` argument, the direct call sites and supporting tests also need updates. `useTimelineCommit.materializeData()` is the main runtime caller, but `render-bounds.validation.test.ts` also calls `rowsToConfig()` directly and would otherwise go stale.

Shot-drop support needs changes in more than `dispatchTimelineDrop()`. `onTimelineDragOver()` currently rejects everything except asset-key, text-tool, effect-layer, file, and generation drag types, and `useExternalDrop.test.tsx` only covers those existing types, so the plan is missing supporting acceptance logic and test updates for the new drag payload.

checks.question: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?

findings.detail: The current `applyEdit({ type: 'config' })` callers all pass resolved clip/track mutations from track management, clip editing, and multi-drag. None of those callers use it for arbitrary config metadata, so treating that branch as a convenient pin/unpin metadata channel would change an existing contract rather than extending an already-matching one.

The `onShotGroupSwitchToFinalVideo` callback passed from `TimelineEditor` expects only `{ shotId, clipIds, rowId }`, and before switching it dismisses the reminder via `finalVideo.id`. Any new menu state for pin/unpin or mode-switching still has to preserve that caller behavior and keep switch-to-video conditioned on actual final-video availability.

`useShotGroups()` has a single production caller in `TimelineEditor`, which currently passes `rows`, `meta`, `registry`, `shots`, and `finalVideoShotIds`. Making `pinnedShotGroups` optional would not force changes on any other production caller because there are none.

checks.question: Does the approach match how the codebase solves similar problems?

findings.detail: The repository already centralizes media insertion rules in `useAssetManagement.handleAssetDrop()` and the external-drop helpers, including track compatibility, manual-track defaults, and clip-order updates. The plan's shot-drop and switch-to-video steps will be more robust if they reuse or extract from those helpers instead of hand-building new clip insertion logic in multiple places.

The current editor state model funnels mutations through immutable `applyEdit()` calls that rebuild `TimelineData` from configs or rows. The plan's alternate idea of updating `pinnedShotGroups` on `dataRef.current.config` before calling `applyEdit()` cuts across that convention and would make undo/history reasoning much harder.

checks.question: Is there convincing verification for the change?

findings.detail: The plan only adds explicit automated tests for `useShotGroups` and serialization, but the riskiest logic changes are in `useSwitchToFinalVideo`, `useClipDrag`, and the config migration/save pipeline. This repo already has focused test harnesses for `useClipDrag`, `useExternalDrop`, `TimelineCanvas`, and save utilities, and the plan should extend those rather than relying mostly on smoke testing.

There is no explicit test item for `migrateToFlatTracks()` or `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry()` preserving `pinnedShotGroups`, even though those helpers are exactly where the new field will be dropped if an edit is missed. A serialization-only round trip is not enough coverage for the runtime edit path.

The plan's AI-agent compatibility check is inspection-oriented rather than test-oriented. Given that `TimelineConfig` is shared with the edge function, a small agent-side test or fixture containing `pinnedShotGroups` would be more convincing than code review alone.

checks.question: Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?

findings.detail: Several `must` criteria are user-visible behaviors with weak automated evidence in the plan, including the solid/dashed border rendering, undo/redo behavior for pin and switch operations, and grouped drag moving all clips together. Those are important goals, but without matching automated checks they are not strong review-gate `must` criteria as written.

`AI timeline agent (supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/) does not break when pinnedShotGroups is present in config` is a reasonable requirement, but the plan does not define a crisp yes/no verification step beyond manual inspection, so it is also weakly specified for a `must` criterion.

Some criteria are well-formed and worth keeping as hard gates. In particular, keeping all existing `useShotGroups` tests green and preserving `pinnedShotGroups` through the full save/load round trip are concrete and objectively reviewable.

flags.concern: shot-groups: suppressing inferred groups by `shotId + trackId` is too coarse and can hide separate inferred clusters for the same shot on one track.
flags.evidence: `useShotGroups()` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts` can flush and restart groups multiple times within the same row for the same shot when gaps or ordering breaks occur, so a single pinned group does not uniquely cover the whole shot/track pair.

flags.concern: timeline-config-mutations: metadata-only pinned-group edits cannot use the current `applyEdit(type='config')` path because that path only accepts `ResolvedTimelineConfig` and serializes only clips/tracks/output/customEffects.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts` lines 46-57 and 226-227 define the config mutation shape and serialize it through `serializeForDisk()`, while `src/tools/video-editor/lib/serialize.ts` lines 124-138 only emit `output`, `tracks`, `clips`, and optional `customEffects`.

flags.concern: clip-drag-selection: auto-expanding a pinned group on pointer-down would regress normal click selection semantics unless expansion is deferred until the drag threshold is crossed.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts` computes `draggedClipIds` during pointer-down, but still uses click-style selection behavior on pointer-up when `session.hasMoved` is false, so eager expansion changes non-drag interaction semantics.

flags.concern: shot-drop-dnd: the repository has no shot drag payload or shared drag type today, so Phase 6 is incomplete unless shared DnD utilities and shot-list/gallery drag sources are updated together.
flags.evidence: `src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts` only defines `generation`, `generation-multi`, `file`, and `none`, and code search found no existing shot drag `setData()`/`getData()` flow for the timeline to consume.

flags.concern: migration-pipeline: normal config migration rebuilds `TimelineConfig` with an allowlist and will drop `pinnedShotGroups` unless `migrateToFlatTracks()` is explicitly updated.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.ts` lines 167-186 rebuild the config object with only `output`, `tracks`, `clips`, and `customEffects`, so the new field would be lost on load and on edit-time migration.
